text: One person that made me feel the magic of coding. And I remember him going like 'Ajke ekta bombastic problem solve korbo'

In terms of teaching he's the GOAT without any doubt. But apart from the teaching part no one can match the gem of a human being sir is. Sir is such a kind soul that when his students didn't do well in an exam sir blames himself and tell 'maybe I did lack in teaching you guys'. But in reality it was fault of the students because we didn't study properly.
KING! GOAT
GOAT PURAI

The best human, mentor, teacher you will ever find in your BracU journey. The man with zero hater! Always ready to help you. You are unlucky if you don't get the chance to meet him & attend his class.
GOAT of all GOATS
Best CSE110 faculty
He is one of the best faculty in BRACU I have ever seen, he is a good human being also, he gives many instruction, which as a freshers I need most.
If you follow his lecture notes closely and ask questions upfront, his insights are genuinely deep. Very approachable on Discord — always responsive.
Moves too fast and relies heavily on slides without simplifying complex logic. Great personality outside class, but classroom delivery needs work.
Polarizing faculty. Brilliant person and super available, but teaching clarity is inconsistent. Works best if you are a self-learner.
Quizzes feel unnecessarily difficult and pacing is chaotic. Outside class he's great — Discord availability is 10/10. High variance experience.
Use his consultation hours and stay on top of the material and it becomes manageable. Not for passive learners at all.
Recent semesters have dropped significantly. Students from 230 are saying run — I would echo that. His past reputation does not reflect the current experience.
One of the hardest courses made harder by fast-paced teaching. Marking is inconsistent — can be lenient one exam, CG crusher the next.
Course notes are dense but valuable. His Discord support is the only reason I passed. Would not take again but respect the effort he puts in outside class.
Generally a good experience. Quizzes are easy but finals are a different beast. Build a good rapport early and she is very supportive.
Teaching is monotonous and speech-heavy. Clear favouritism in marking — if she likes you, grade bumps happen. If not, expect a tough time.
She was genuinely great with me — supportive, fair marks, and always willing to help in labs. Course load is very manageable.
High-risk high-reward. Her class is a split experience — some students rave, some walk away feeling targeted. Stay on her good side.
Labs are fine and she genuinely helps out during sessions. Lectures can drag but you cover what you need. Watch for inconsistency in marking.
Described as angel by some and CG destroyer by others — both are accurate depending on which side you fall on. Average teaching, politics-heavy marking.
Reads directly from slides and has a weak grasp of advanced Java. Her notes and past papers are excellent but class attendance barely helps.
Mocked a student for asking a question during consultation. Behavior is unacceptable. Below-average teaching. The only saving grace is the past paper collection.
Once praised as goated and pookie, but the last two semesters have been a completely different person. Volatile, mocking, and unpredictable. Proceed with extreme caution.
Run fly or die — the warning is real. Her resources are the best in the department for CSE220 but the emotional toll of her behavior is not worth it for most students.
For a first programming course, clarity is everything and she does not deliver it. Uses ChatGPT-generated code examples sometimes which confuses more than it helps.
Behavior is the biggest concern. Known to be dismissive of female students and harsh to anyone she perceives as not trying. Not a comfortable learning environment.

FFR is the GOAT for CSE250. Teaching is 11/10 — makes circuits feel simple by focusing on theory before problems. Quizzes are directly from slides and he takes multiple, counting the best few. Responds on Discord even late at night. S-tier faculty.

Took FFR for CSE250 and it was the best decision. He guides you on exactly what to expect in exams. Marking is so generous it is almost impossible to lose marks needlessly. Assignments are straightforward. A gem and an angel reincarnation according to students.

He genuinely wants his students to succeed. Classes are highly engaging and he is humble and supportive. He puts major effort into teaching every single class. For many students FFR turns CSE250 from a challenging course into an enjoyable experience.

RAO is absolute 10/10 for CSE250. His hand-drawn notes start from the basics and include comprehensive practice problems with solutions. Complex circuit concepts become intuitive. He is passionate, organized, and responds quickly online.

Mohammed Radoan is a GOAT faculty. Quizzes have bonus systems like 4 questions where only 3 count. Marking prioritizes correct process over final answer — if steps are right he gives near-full marks even if the final calculation is off. His notes alone are enough to score well.

RAO provides a clear roadmap for exam preparation. He is humble, friendly, and genuinely cares about student well-being. He gives multiple quizzes and counts only the best, offering great recovery chances. A top-priority choice for CSE250.

TAV is a GOAT and pookie faculty for CSE250. He records his own videos, provides extensive resources, and explains everything crystal clear. Bonus marks throughout the semester are a lifesaver. Highly recommended for hardware courses.

Tarvir Anjum Aditto puts massive effort into teaching. His handnotes and recorded videos make the course very manageable. He is patient with repeated questions and does not mind explaining the same thing multiple times. As long as your script is not blank marking is very generous.

One of the most liked younger lecturers in CSE. TAV is approachable, considerate, and creates a supportive environment. Bonus marks have saved many grades. Consistently called best faculty and pookie by students across multiple semesters.

AKDB is amazing for CSE250. His marking is exceptional — students exaggerate it as 100/10. He offers ungraded bonus assignments that improve your score while reinforcing circuits. Lab vivas are stress-free. Humble, chill, and genuinely cares about students.

Some find his teaching average but his marking more than compensates. He is patient and provides practice circuits. First quiz can be hard due to time constraints but he adjusts later quizzes to be easier. Very helpful on Discord if you cannot attend early consultation hours.

AKDB contacts students personally to ensure they understand the material. No bias and a very supportive relationship with students. Lenient in lab vivas and reports. A long-time lab favorite who has transitioned into theory courses and been warmly received.

SDS is a Legend and GOAT. Teaching is absolute 10/10 — covers the full syllabus punctually and breaks down complex circuit theories into simple logical steps. Marking strictly follows rubrics with partial marks where deserved. A 4.0 is achievable if you attend and follow his instructions.

Shadman Shahriar treats students like family members. He is one of the best human beings at BRACU. His in-class explanations are so refined that you barely need extra study at home if you pay attention. Fair and transparent marking with no free marks but no unfair deductions.

Teaching boss with deep knowledge. Comfortable environment for questions. Called pookie, gem, and great human by students. Strictly follows rubrics so no chance of free marks but also no unfair deductions. One of the most knowledgeable and passionate faculties in CSE.

AQT is one of the most student-friendly faculties in the department. In lab settings she is clear and very helpful. Marking is consistently lenient and fair. Behavior is absolute 10/10. Students call her pookie and best of best. Highly recommended for hardware courses.

Aniqa Tabassum creates a low-stress environment especially in labs. Some find theory lectures for CSE250 average but her generous marking more than compensates. Very comfortable to approach with any problem. Great choice for students who prioritize a supportive atmosphere.

AQT does not create unnecessary pressure. Her marking for CSE250 makes the course much easier to manage compared to stricter faculties. She is exceptionally kind and students feel comfortable asking questions. A top-tier choice for lab-based hardware courses.

NFS is one of the best lab faculties. He explains concepts clearly, is patient with all queries, and creates a completely stress-free environment. Students affectionately call him pookie and Need for Speed. Very approachable, humble, and a genuine good guy.

Nafis Faisal is a top choice for lab-based courses. His grading is fair and student-friendly. He does not penalize unnecessarily and is very easy to communicate with. A supportive mentor that students keep recommending across multiple semesters.

NFS is especially recommended for hands-on lab courses. He explains concepts in a way that is easy to understand and patiently works through student confusion. His humble nature makes him one of the most popular adjunct faculties in the department.

MHY has mixed reviews for CSE250. Teaching is technically solid with detailed hand-notes. However he can be strict and intimidating, scolding students who cannot answer questions in class. Lack of script review makes marking feel unclear to many students.

Abu Musa Al Ashary is technically knowledgeable but his classroom behavior is rigid. He speaks softly making him hard to follow. Some report little or no partial credit for calculation errors. Expect a high-risk experience. Watch YouTube playlists before class to avoid being unprepared.

MHY expects perfect execution of calculations until the end. He asks random questions in class and may scold unprepared students. Despite technical expertise many describe the experience as high-risk. Be very well prepared before attending his section.
